title: When Safety Increases: - Assess Risks: Ask,
subtitle: Balance Safety and Risk
description:
When safety increases:
- Assess risks: Ask, "Am I compensating for safety by taking unnecessary risks?"
- Set boundaries: Stick to cautious habits even in safer environments.
- Focus on long-term impact: Remember that short-term safety doesn’t eliminate long-term consequences.

Example: Driving a car with advanced safety features? Avoid speeding just because you feel more protected.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify one recent or likely safety upgrade in your life. Write a one-sentence anchor rule to maintain a precaution, such as setting a speed limit or keeping a spare key hidden.

howTo.title: Ask the critical questions
howTo.summary: Whenever you encounter increased safety, ask yourself three micro-questions: How am I feeling? What behavior changed? What small rule can I implement to preserve long-term outcomes?

howTo.title: Implement the counter-rule
howTo.summary: Choose one small, measurable counter-rule based on your reflections. Log this action and its impact on your behavior to ensure accountability and track progress.

howTo.title: Review and adjust your approach
howTo.summary: After a week, assess the effectiveness of your counter-rules. Decide whether to keep, adjust, or remove the anchor based on your experiences and outcomes.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and maintain accountability. Access it at https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/cognitive-biases-how-to-when-safety-increases-assess-risks-ask-am-i/.
